meta_title: Impact Doors in Golf, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Golf, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Call for a free estimate today!
h1: Impact Doors Built for Golf, FL Homes — Strength You Can Count On Every Hurricane Season
intro:
Golf, Florida is one of Palm Beach County's most understated gems — a small, tight-knit village community tucked between Boynton Beach and Delray Beach where well-maintained homes average around $390,000 and neighbors take pride in where they live. But beneath the quiet charm of Golf Village lies a very real vulnerability that every homeowner in the 33436 ZIP code understands: South Florida's hurricane season doesn't discriminate by zip code, and a community located just 8 miles from the Atlantic coastline can still experience devastating wind speeds, flying debris, and storm surge pressure that can tear an unprotected door right off its frame. That's why investing in certified impact doors isn't a luxury here — it's a fundamental part of responsible homeownership.

With approximately 60% of homes in Golf constructed before 1994 — the year Florida's building codes were dramatically overhauled in response to Hurricane Andrew — a large share of the housing stock was built when standard hollow-core or single-pane glass doors were considered acceptable. Those doors are not rated for the 140 mph wind zone that Golf sits within, and they certainly weren't engineered to handle the kinds of wind-borne debris events that Hurricanes Frances, Wilma, and Irma delivered to Palm Beach County. Replacing aging entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors with modern, hurricane-rated impact products is one of the most impactful upgrades a Golf homeowner can make — both for safety and for long-term home value.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've built our reputation as 'The Kings of Service' by delivering exactly what communities like Golf need: expertly installed, code-compliant impact door systems that perform when it matters most. Our team has completed work in more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we bring that depth of local knowledge directly to every project in Golf. Whether you're upgrading a single front entry door or replacing every exterior door opening in your Golf Village home, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides the craftsmanship, product quality, and customer care that this community deserves.

section_why.h2: Why Golf Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Hurricane Door Installation
section_why.body:
Choosing the right contractor for impact door installation in Golf isn't just about price — it's about trusting the people who will be working on your home to get it right the first time. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing stands apart from the competition in a few critical ways that matter deeply to discerning homeowners in this community. First and foremost, every installation we perform is done entirely by our own in-house crews. We do not subcontract. That means the same experienced technicians who carry our company name are the ones measuring your openings, removing your old doors, and setting your new impact units to manufacturer and code specifications. With 60+ years of combined experience across our installation team, this isn't a workforce learning on the job.

Our commitment to in-house work also means seamless accountability. When you call Premier Impact Windows & Roofing after your installation, you're talking to the same team that did the work — not a third-party subcontractor whose warranty obligations may be murky. For Golf Village homeowners who take pride in their properties and expect a certain level of professionalism, this distinction is significant. We've seen far too many South Florida residents deal with the aftermath of a rushed or improperly permitted door installation, only to discover during a wind mitigation inspection or insurance audit that their products weren't installed correctly.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau, and we're fully licensed and insured to operate throughout Palm Beach County. We handle the permit process through the Village of Golf Building Department from start to finish, so you never have to chase paperwork or schedule inspections yourself. We know the local inspectors, we understand the Village's requirements, and we've navigated this process dozens of times. From the initial free estimate to the final inspection sign-off, we make the entire experience straightforward and stress-free. That's what being The Kings of Service actually means in practice.

section_history.h2: Golf's Storm History Makes a Compelling Case for Impact Doors
section_history.body:
The Village of Golf may be small, but its storm history is anything but insignificant. Since 1990, Golf and the surrounding Palm Beach County region have been affected by at least 7 named storm events, with three standing out as particularly destructive to the area's housing stock. Hurricane Frances made landfall in September 2004 as a Category 2 storm, bringing sustained winds and prolonged battering that tested the endurance of every door, window, and roof in the county. Less than a year later, Hurricane Wilma arrived in October 2005 as one of the fastest-moving and most intense storms to ever cross South Florida, generating wind gusts that stripped doors and shutters from homes throughout the region. Many Golf homeowners who survived Frances with minimal damage found Wilma to be a far more destructive event.

Then came Hurricane Irma in September 2017 — a storm that reminded an entire new generation of Palm Beach County residents that no neighborhood is truly immune from major hurricane impacts. Irma's outer bands produced sustained tropical storm to Category 1 force winds across the county for hours, and homes without impact-rated doors or windows experienced broken glass, water intrusion, and in some cases, catastrophic structural compromise due to pressure differentials caused by breached openings. The pattern across all three storms is consistent: homes with impact-rated doors and windows fared dramatically better than those relying on traditional doors paired with removable shutters.

For the roughly 60% of Golf homes built before the post-Andrew code reforms, these storms represent a clear and documented warning. Older door frames, aging jambs, and original hardware were not engineered for 140 mph wind loads. Upgrading to certified impact doors is the most direct way for Golf homeowners to address that vulnerability before the next named storm forms in the Atlantic.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements Every Golf Homeowner Should Understand
section_code.body:
Golf, Florida falls within Palm Beach County's jurisdiction for Florida Building Code compliance, and while the Village is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies primarily to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal areas — it is still subject to stringent hurricane-rated product requirements under the current Florida Building Code. Specifically, all exterior doors installed in Golf must meet the requirements for a 140 mph design wind speed, which means every product must carry the appropriate impact resistance certification demonstrating it can withstand both large and small missile impacts as defined by FBC testing standards.

For homeowners in Golf, this means that not every impact door sold at a big-box retailer will be compliant. Products must carry a valid Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Florida Product Approval number that specifically covers the wind speed and exposure category applicable to your home's location. Sliding glass doors, French doors, and entry doors all have distinct certification requirements, and the installation method — including proper anchoring into the rough opening frame — is just as critical as the product itself. An improperly installed door that uses a code-approved product can still fail a wind mitigation inspection and may not be covered under your insurance claim after a storm.

Permit applications for impact door installations in Golf are processed through the Village of Golf Building Department. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this entire process on your behalf, from submitting the permit application with the correct product approval documentation to scheduling the required inspections. We are intimately familiar with the Village's process and timeline, which helps keep your project on schedule. All completed installations receive a final inspection sign-off, giving you the documentation you need for your insurance wind mitigation report — a critical step toward unlocking the premium discounts that Palm Beach County homeowners are entitled to.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Products Built for South Florida's Toughest Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most respected impact door brands in the South Florida market: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. These are not commodity products — they are purpose-engineered hurricane protection systems designed to perform in the exact conditions that Golf homeowners face each season. ESW's impact door lineup includes entry doors, sliding glass door systems, and French door configurations, all featuring laminated impact-resistant glass bonded with an interlayer that holds the glass intact even when struck by wind-borne debris. The frames are engineered to flex without compromising the seal, and multi-point locking systems are standard — distributing locking force across multiple points along the door's height rather than relying on a single latch point that can be forced under pressure.

CWI products offer Golf homeowners equally impressive performance credentials, with door systems that carry Florida Product Approval for high wind zones and are engineered for both impact resistance and long-term energy efficiency. In South Florida's heat and humidity, a well-insulated impact door doesn't just protect against storms — it actively reduces your air conditioning load by limiting heat transfer through the door opening. For a Golf Village home averaging $390,000 in value, these are products that enhance both the safety profile and the market appeal of the property.

Both ESW and CWI door systems are available in a wide range of configurations, finishes, and glass options to complement the architectural character of homes in Golf. Whether your home features a classic colonial entry, a contemporary sliding glass wall to a backyard patio, or elegant French doors opening to a Florida room, we can source and install a product that meets both your aesthetic preferences and your hurricane protection requirements. Our team will walk you through every option during your free in-home consultation.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Golf — And How to Finance the Investment
section_cost.body:
For homeowners in Golf where property values average approximately $390,000, impact door installation represents a smart, value-preserving investment rather than a discretionary expense. The actual cost of impact door replacement varies based on the number of openings, door type (entry, sliding, French), size, and product selection. A single impact entry door installation typically ranges from $1,500 to $3,500 installed, while larger sliding glass door systems or multi-panel French door configurations can run from $3,000 to $7,000 or more per opening depending on size and complexity. A full exterior door replacement project covering all openings in a typical Golf Village home might fall anywhere between $8,000 and $20,000 depending on scope.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes this investment accessible through flexible financing options that start at just $87 per month, with $0 down programs available for qualified homeowners. We work with lending partners who understand the South Florida home improvement market, and our team will help you identify the financing structure that fits your budget. When you factor in the insurance premium savings available to Golf homeowners who complete a wind mitigation upgrade — which can range from 15% to 45% on the windstorm portion of your homeowner's insurance — the net monthly cost of financing new impact doors can be surprisingly manageable.

Beyond insurance savings, consider the return on investment from a property value perspective. Real estate professionals consistently identify hurricane impact protection upgrades as among the highest-ROI home improvements in South Florida. For a $390,000 home in Golf, a professionally installed, permitted impact door system adds measurable resale value, increases buyer appeal, and eliminates the annual cost and labor burden of installing and removing hurricane shutters. The math on impact doors in this market is compelling from every angle.

section_insurance.h2: Unlock Real Insurance Savings with Impact Doors in Palm Beach County
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediately tangible benefits of upgrading to certified impact doors in Golf is the reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums — specifically the windstorm and hurricane coverage component, which in Palm Beach County represents a substantial portion of most homeowners' annual insurance cost. Florida's My Safe Florida Home program and the standard wind mitigation inspection process both recognize impact-rated doors as qualifying protective features that reduce a home's risk profile. When a licensed wind mitigation inspector verifies that your home has properly permitted and installed impact-rated opening protections, that report is submitted to your insurance carrier as the basis for premium credits.

Major carriers operating in Palm Beach County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Property & Casualty, and others — apply wind mitigation credits that can translate to hundreds or even thousands of dollars in annual premium savings, depending on your coverage amount and the extent of your impact upgrades. For a Golf homeowner paying $4,000 to $7,000 or more per year in combined homeowner's insurance, a 20% to 40% reduction in the wind premium component is a meaningful and recurring financial benefit that compounds year after year.

It's important to note that the insurance savings are maximized when all exterior openings — not just the doors — carry impact protection. However, even a partial upgrade, such as replacing only your impact doors, can qualify you for partial credits under most carrier schedules. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all the documentation you need after your installation, including product approval numbers, the permit and final inspection records from the Village of Golf Building Department, and guidance on requesting a wind mitigation inspection from a qualified inspector. We want to make sure you capture every dollar of savings you've earned by investing in your home's protection.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Golf, FL if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. While Golf is approximately 8 miles from the Atlantic coastline, it sits within a 140 mph design wind zone under the Florida Building Code. Hurricanes Frances, Wilma, and Irma all produced damaging winds well inland from the coast across Palm Beach County. Wind-borne debris from neighboring properties, trees, and infrastructure becomes a serious threat at these wind speeds. Standard doors are not engineered to handle these conditions, making impact-rated doors a critical protection measure regardless of your distance from the water.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Golf, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door installation costs in Golf typically range from $1,500 to $3,500 for a single entry door and $3,000 to $7,000+ for sliding glass or French door systems. A whole-home door replacement project can range from $8,000 to $20,000 depending on the number and type of openings. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners, making it possible to upgrade your home's protection without a large upfront payment.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace doors in the Village of Golf?
faqs.a: Yes. Impact door replacements in Golf require a building permit through the Village of Golf Building Department. The permit application must include the Florida Product Approval number for the specific door product being installed, as well as installation drawings confirming the anchoring method and wind load compliance. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf, from application submission through final inspection. This documentation is also essential for your wind mitigation inspection and insurance discount qualification.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take in Golf?
faqs.a: For most Golf homeowners, the actual installation of impact doors is completed in one to two days once the permit has been issued. The permitting process through the Village of Golf Building Department typically takes one to three weeks, depending on current application volume. We schedule your installation date once the permit is approved, and our in-house crews work efficiently to minimize disruption to your household. Final inspections are typically scheduled within a week of installation completion.

faqs.q: Will impact doors actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Golf?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases they will. Once your impact doors are installed and permitted, you can request a wind mitigation inspection from a licensed inspector. The resulting report documents your home's opening protections and is submitted to your insurance carrier for premium credit consideration. Palm Beach County homeowners with fully protected openings commonly see windstorm premium reductions of 15% to 45%, which on a typical policy can mean hundreds to thousands of dollars in annual savings. Over time, these savings can substantially offset your installation investment.

cta_body: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is ready to provide a free, no-obligation estimate for impact door installation at your Golf, FL home — call us today and experience why we're The Kings of Service throughout Palm Beach County.
